step.text: File NDH-4 for Nidhi declaration within 1 year
step.name: Step 4

mainEntity.name: Can a Nidhi Company accept deposits from non-members?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Nidhi Companies can only accept deposits from and lend to their own members. They cannot deal with non-members.
Minimum 200 members within 1 year of incorporation. Net owned funds: ₹10 lakhs. Ratio of NOF to deposits: 1:20.

Mutual benefit society under Section 406 of Companies Act. Members save and borrow from the company. Cannot deal with non-members. Governed by Nidhi Rules 2014. Similar to cooperative bank but registered as company.

mainEntity.name: What are the requirements for Nidhi company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Minimum 200 members within 1 year, net owned funds ₹20 lakhs, 1:20 NOF to deposit ratio, unencumbered term deposits minimum 10% of outstanding deposits. 3 directors required.

mainEntity.name: Can Nidhi company accept deposits from non-members?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Nidhi can only accept deposits from and lend to its members. Cannot issue debentures, cannot acquire another company, and cannot deal in any securities.

mainEntity.name: What is the lending limit for Nidhi company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Maximum loan: ₹15 lakhs per member. Maximum against fixed deposit: 100% of deposit. Against other specified securities: limits apply. Interest rate: maximum 7.5% above highest rate offered on deposits.

mainEntity.name: Is FDI allowed in Nidhi company?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Nidhi companies are in the negative list for FDI. No foreign investment permitted. Only Indian residents can be members and shareholders.
